name: Mcginn Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Sargent/Cogliano/Fuerst holding group (also owns Plymouth & Brockton, Brush Hill Transportation, Blue Apple Bus, Massachusetts Transportation Co.)
principals: Winthrop Sargent (51%), John Cogliano (30%), Paul Fuerst (19%); acquired from George S. Anzuoni and Richard W. Anzuoni in 2019
districts_or_customers: Boston North Shore motorcoach/charter; primarily charter and commuter, limited school bus exposure
recent_news: 2019: Sargent/Cogliano/Fuerst acquired control from Anzuoni family alongside Plymouth & Brockton and Brush Hill Transportation, consolidating MA motorcoach operations under a single ownership group.
fit_notes: Not a fit — primarily a motorcoach/charter operator (not yellow school bus), already consolidated into a multi-company strategic holding group post-2019 acquisition. Address in the source record (Plymouth, MA) is for the affiliated Plymouth & Brockton entity; McGinn HQ is Lynn, MA.

name: Mnsota School Bus Tech LLC
owner_or_parent: North America Central School Bus (NACSB) — majority-owned by H.I.G. Capital (since Aug 2021); minority investors Gryphon Investors and Goldner Hawn
districts_or_customers: NACSB MN operating subsidiary (Madelia depot); part of NACSB platform serving 120+ communities across IL, IN, WI, MO, KS, PA, ID, IA, TX, MN and Ontario with 5,700+ vehicles
recent_news: 2021: H.I.G. Capital acquired NACSB from Gryphon Investors/Goldner Hawn, who retained minority stakes; NACSB remains 4th/5th-largest school bus operator in North America and continues to grow via tuck-ins.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in target — already a PE-owned platform subsidiary (NACSB/H.I.G. Capital). NACSB is itself a strategic acquirer competing with Summit/Beacon in the consolidation play.

name: Drogin Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Drogin family (historically); current operating status unclear
principals: Drogin family (historical Boulevard Transit Lines operators)
districts_or_customers: Historically operated Hudson County NJ/NYC commuter routes (Bayonne-Journal Square #10, 99/99S, 144, 122 lines) under Boulevard Transit Lines; not documented as a yellow-bus school contractor
recent_news: Boulevard Transit Lines/Drogin family operations went out of business ~late 1990s–2000/2001 when Coach USA/Red and Tan took over the routes. A 'Drogin Bus Company' charter listing at 53 Kennedy Blvd persists in directory data but with no verified operating presence; appears largely defunct or dormant.
fit_notes: Not a fit — historical Hudson County commuter/transit operator (not school bus), and the operating company appears defunct since ~2001. The active 'Drogin Bus Co' listing looks like a stale directory entry rather than a live school transportation business.

name: Trobecs Bus Svc
principals: Bethany Bertram (CEO/President/co-owner); founded by Tony, Frances and Joe Trobec in 1938; transitioned in 1980s to Tom Trobec, Betty Trobec and Emily Gohman
districts_or_customers: Central MN — St. Cloud / St. Joseph / Holdingford / St. Stephen area school districts; mix of school routes and motorcoach charters (weddings, corporate, athletics)
recent_news: 85+ years in operation as of 2023; multi-generational family ownership now in third generation under Bethany Bertram. Active member of MSBOA; recognized in MN pupil transportation industry.
fit_notes: Plausible tuck-in — independent, multi-generational family operator in Central MN with school + charter mix. Third-generation ownership and 85+ year history suggest medium succession signal; size (~58 employees, ~$2.4M est. revenue) is small but consolidatable. Worth a direct outreach.

name: L & S Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Primary contract: Queen Anne's County Public Schools (MD upper Eastern Shore); also private schools and charter groups (clubs, churches, civic organizations)
recent_news: No M&A or sale news identified; long-tenured single-county school contractor on the Eastern Shore with a stable fleet of ~15 buses.
fit_notes: Plausible small tuck-in — independent, owner-operated since 1996 with the Queen Anne's County contract anchor and Eastern Shore footprint adjacent to Summit's MD/DE footprint. Single-principal succession profile (Lawrence Chanaud) is a moderate signal. Small (~15 buses) but a clean bolt-on for a MD platform.
